ip_outcome_note: ip_verified already true in frontmatter; no IP work required.

trivia_bullets.text: Chandra's Pyreling is original to Core Set 2021 (reprint: false per Scryfall) and was previewed by Wizards of the Coast on June 9, 2020. It is one of two named Chandra-companion creatures in M21 alongside Chandra's Magmutt (137). M21 has no narrative story spotlights — Chandra's presence is mechanical and thematic, not story-chapter driven. [Scryfall: previewed_at=2020-06-09, story_spotlight=false]

EDHREC rank 11,964 [Scryfall: edhrec_rank]. A 1/3 Uncommon for {1}{R} that gains +1/+0 and double strike whenever any source you control deals noncombat damage to an opponent. The trigger includes planeswalker ping abilities, Guttersnipe-style triggers, and burn spells — allowing the Pyreling to grow and double-strike in the same turn as a Lightning Bolt. NOT legal in Pauper (uncommon). [Scryfall: legalities, pauper=not_legal]

"I think Chandra just wanted something to blame random scorch marks on." — Jace Beleren. [Scryfall] The quote is a characteristic piece of Jace-Chandra banter — their long-running antagonistic-turned-collegial relationship across multiple sets is one of the most documented inter-planeswalker dynamics in MTG lore. Jace's dry tone here frames Chandra's pyromancy as casually destructive rather than malicious.

Community sentiment on TappedOut: Chandra's Pyreling is frequently compared to Chandra's Spitfire from M10, described as 'equally devastating but lacking evasion.' The card's combo ceiling in noncombat-damage decks (Torbran, Thane of Red Fell; Guttersnipe) is noted; Break Through the Line is cited as a frequent evasion patch. [TappedOut: chandras-pyreling card page]

related_cards.name: Chandra's Magmutt
related_cards.note: The paired Chandra-companion common in M21

related_cards.name: Chandra's Spitfire
related_cards.note: Direct mechanical predecessor — same trigger type (noncombat damage → pump), frequently compared in community discussions

related_cards.name: Torbran, Thane of Red Fell
related_cards.note: Strongest EDH synergy partner — Torbran's damage buff triggers Pyreling's noncombat damage condition

related_cards.name: Guttersnipe
related_cards.note: Spell-damage engine that repeatedly triggers Pyreling in spell-heavy builds
